00029 
00030 #include <types.h>
00031 #include <kern/errno.h>
00032 #include <lib.h>
00033 #include <addrspace.h>
00034 #include <vm.h>
00035 
00036 /*
00037  * Note! If OPT_DUMBVM is set, as is the case until you start the VM
00038  * assignment, this file is not compiled or linked or in any way
00039  * used. The cheesy hack versions in dumbvm.c are used instead.
00040  */
00041 
00042 struct addrspace *
00043 as_create(void)
00044 {
00045         struct addrspace *as;
00046 
00047         as = kmalloc(sizeof(struct addrspace));
00048         if (as == NULL) {
00049                 return NULL;
00050         }
00051 
00052         /*
00053          * Initialize as needed.
00054          */
00055 
00056         return as;
00057 }
00058 
00059 int
00060 as_copy(struct addrspace *old, struct addrspace **ret)
00061 {
00062         struct addrspace *newas;
00063 
00064         newas = as_create();
00065         if (newas==NULL) {
00066                 return ENOMEM;
00067         }
00068 
00069         /*
00070          * Write this.
00071          */
00072 
00073         (void)old;
00074         
00075         *ret = newas;
00076         return 0;
00077 }
00078 
00079 void
00080 as_destroy(struct addrspace *as)
00081 {
00082         /*
00083          * Clean up as needed.
00084          */
00085         
00086         kfree(as);
00087 }
00088 
00089 void
00090 as_activate(struct addrspace *as)
00091 {
00092         /*
00093          * Write this.
00094          */
00095 
00096         (void)as;  // suppress warning until code gets written
00097 }
00098 
00099 /*
00100  * Set up a segment at virtual address VADDR of size MEMSIZE. The
00101  * segment in memory extends from VADDR up to (but not including)
00102  * VADDR+MEMSIZE.
00103  *
00104  * The READABLE, WRITEABLE, and EXECUTABLE flags are set if read,
00105  * write, or execute permission should be set on the segment. At the
00106  * moment, these are ignored. When you write the VM system, you may
00107  * want to implement them.
00108  */
00109 int
00110 as_define_region(struct addrspace *as, vaddr_t vaddr, size_t sz,
00111                  int readable, int writeable, int executable)
00112 {
00113         /*
00114          * Write this.
00115          */
00116 
00117         (void)as;
00118         (void)vaddr;
00119         (void)sz;
00120         (void)readable;
00121         (void)writeable;
00122         (void)executable;
00123         return EUNIMP;
00124 }
00125 
00126 int
00127 as_prepare_load(struct addrspace *as)
00128 {
00129         /*
00130          * Write this.
00131          */
00132 
00133         (void)as;
00134         return 0;
00135 }
00136 
00137 int
00138 as_complete_load(struct addrspace *as)
00139 {
00140         /*
00141          * Write this.
00142          */
00143 
00144         (void)as;
00145         return 0;
00146 }
00147 
00148 int
00149 as_define_stack(struct addrspace *as, vaddr_t *stackptr)
00150 {
00151         /*
00152          * Write this.
00153          */
00154 
00155         (void)as;
00156 
00157         /* Initial user-level stack pointer */
00158         *stackptr = USERSTACK;
00159         
00160         return 0;
00161 }
00162
